match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy, SCARLETT in light casual beachwear suitable for sun and sand sits at a pristine white vanity inside a bright coastal modern studio with open shutters, her fingers lifting the coral lipstick from its crisp white box and turning it slowly to catch the light, warm morning sunlight cutting sharply through the shutters to cast clean directional shadows across the vanity surface and illuminate the matte champagne-silver body of the BROADCAST_MIC mounted before her, high-key airy atmosphere with golden hour warmth, shallow depth of field softly blurring the background shutters and walls while keeping the lipstick and hands crisp, eye-level cinematic framing with true-to-life color grading and slight warmth in highlights, clean natural window light and minimal processing for an effortless fresh mood

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: INT. BONDI STUDIO - MORNING - Bright studio space with white vanity and open shutters letting sunlight stream in
    - Image 4 [ELEMENT REF]: BROADCAST_MIC - A large-diaphragm condenser studio microphone with a matte champagne/silver metallic cylindrical body
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
